source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The figure shows seven regions enclosed by three circles. We call two regions neighbouring if their boundaries have more than one common point. In each region a number is written. The number in any region is equal to the sum of the numbers of its neighbouring regions. Two of the numbers are shown. What number is written in the central region? <image1>
extraction_notes: `decoded_image` is an object placeholder, so `image` was used as the question image path.
An additional image URL appears inside `solution` and was included in `image_paths`.

image_dependency: The question explicitly refers to 'The figure' and depends on the layout of seven regions formed by three circles
Neighbouring regions are defined by shared boundaries, so region adjacency and the location of the two shown numbers must be read from the image
multi_step: Solving requires extracting the region adjacency structure from the diagram before any algebraic reasoning
The condition that each region equals the sum of its neighbouring regions creates a coupled system across multiple regions
The target is the central region, so the solver must combine diagram interpretation with multi-constraint deduction rather than a single lookup
verifiability: `answer_type` is numeric and `answer_text` is a single value ('0'), which is easy to compare against a predicted result
The underlying reasoning can be checked by reconstructing the adjacency graph from the image and verifying the sum equations
`registry_passed` is true, the image asset exists, and the image readability score is reasonably high (0.8479)
